best_rules_serialized.name: Specific Court Departments and Senates
best_rules_serialized.description: Matches specific German court names and abbreviations that were previously in the long list, now using a more structured approach for common courts and abbreviations.

best_rules_serialized.name: Court with Location Genitive
best_rules_serialized.description: Matches court names in genitive case (e.g., 'des Landgerichts') but extracts only the court name, handling compound state names correctly (e.g., 'Sachsen-Anhalt', 'Nordrhein-Westfalen').

best_rules_serialized.name: Organization with Location/Type
best_rules_serialized.description: Matches organizations with specific descriptors like 'Schulzentrum für Technik' or 'Senat des ...', ensuring full capture.

best_rules_serialized.name: Quoted Organization Names
best_rules_serialized.description: Matches organization names enclosed in German quotation marks, but only when preceded by context indicating an organization (e.g., 'Firma', 'Marke', 'Name', 'der', 'des') to avoid matching product names or streets.

best_rules_serialized.name: Specific Court Abbreviations and Full Names
best_rules_serialized.description: Matches common German court abbreviations (BVerfG, BGH, BFH, etc.) with strict word boundaries to avoid partial matches and false positives.

best_rules_serialized.name: Generic Organization Patterns
best_rules_serialized.description: Matches generic organization patterns like 'Firma X GmbH', 'Senat des Y', 'Bundesministerium', 'Landesamt', etc., using structural patterns to generalize.
